spec_match.rationale: The lab now demonstrates the headline behavior: shadow-root @container rules match a host container name declared in the outer document, including direct and nested component cases.

interactive.rationale: Visitors can resize the host container, swap matching and mismatching container-name tokens, switch between one and two shadow-root depths, rerun the live probe, and inspect live computed results.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede now repeats the feature-index promise that container-name matching ignores tree scope for @container queries and frames the result around design-system components.

edge_cases.rationale: The page includes visible feature detection, a cross-tree probe, and an intentional mismatched-token branch that shows how the component falls back when no named container matches.

references.rationale: The concept links directly to the required ChromeStatus entry for feature 5194034339512320 and the CSS Conditional 5 container-name section.

design_system.rationale: The static-card pattern was replaced with a stage, controls, live readouts, and token-based CSS; local button variants set both background and color.

summary: Design System Container is now an interactive shadow-DOM lab for non-tree-scoped container-name matching, with nested component coverage, token-mismatch fallback, live detection, corrected references, and no remaining critique tasks.
